assertions.title: First-time setup reaches live thread browsing end to end

steps.action: Launch the first-run pairing flow, submit a valid manual QR payload, and confirm trust.
steps.expected: The app pairs without hidden setup steps and opens the live Threads surface.
steps.observed: The harness navigated from Pair your phone to this Mac into Threads after Scan pairing QR, Submit scanned payload, and Confirm trust.

steps.action: Wait for the paired thread list to render thread-123.
steps.expected: A usable live thread list appears immediately after pairing.
steps.observed: The test found the thread summary card for thread-123 on the Threads screen.

steps.action: Emit a live thread status change for thread-123.
steps.expected: The live thread list remains usable and reflects the update.
steps.observed: The list updated to show Completed and the session token remained stored.

evidence.consoleErrors: none observed in the expanded Flutter test log
evidence.network: No real network capture; the integration harness used fake pairing and thread APIs plus in-memory bridge events.

assertions.title: Foreground approval notifications surface the correct thread and approval context

steps.action: Open Device settings from the paired Threads surface.
steps.expected: The app remains active on a visible user surface before the approval event arrives.
steps.observed: The harness navigated successfully to Device settings.

steps.action: Emit an approvalRequested event for approval-123 on thread-123 while the app is active.
steps.expected: A foreground notification surfaces approval and thread context.
steps.observed: The UI showed foreground notification text beginning with Approval requested:.

steps.action: Tap Open on the surfaced approval notification.
steps.expected: The matching approval detail context opens.
steps.observed: Approval detail opened and rendered approval-123 for the expected thread context.

evidence.consoleErrors: none observed in the expanded Flutter test log
evidence.network: No real websocket or HTTP capture; the harness injected a fake approvalRequested BridgeEventEnvelope into the live stream.

assertions.title: Foreground live-activity notifications surface the correct thread context

steps.action: Open Device settings from the paired Threads surface.
steps.expected: The app remains in an active foreground state before the live activity event arrives.
steps.observed: The harness reached Device settings and stayed in-app.

steps.action: Emit a messageDelta live activity event for thread-123.
steps.expected: A foreground live-activity notification surfaces the matching thread context.
steps.observed: The UI showed foreground notification text beginning with Live activity update:.

steps.action: Tap Open on the surfaced live-activity notification.
steps.observed: Thread detail opened and displayed thread-123.

evidence.consoleErrors: none observed in the expanded Flutter test log
evidence.network: No real notification transport capture; the harness injected a fake messageDelta BridgeEventEnvelope and validated in-app routing.

assertions.title: Reconnect and foreground resume do not create duplicate or stale notifications or items

steps.action: Emit a stale approval notification and invoke its Open action.
steps.expected: Stale notification handling must not reopen an invalid approval context as if it were still current.
steps.observed: The app stayed on Device settings, did not open Approval detail, and showed a no longer actionable message.

steps.action: Emit duplicate evt-dedup-1 live activity deliveries across the reconnect-style dedup path.
steps.expected: Duplicate actionable notifications/items are suppressed to a single current entry.
steps.observed: The runtime notification state kept exactly one recent notification for eventId evt-dedup-1.

evidence.consoleErrors: none observed in the expanded Flutter test logs
evidence.network: No real websocket capture; the harness injected stale approval and duplicate messageDelta events directly into fake runtime notification state.

frictions.description: Flutter detected multiple attached devices, so each run needed an explicit -d emulator-5554 target to stay inside the emulator-based isolation boundary instead of using a physical phone.
frictions.resolution: Re-ran all targeted commands with -d emulator-5554 and kept the runs serialized.

frictions.description: The assigned integration harnesses produce expanded command logs but do not emit standalone screenshots, videos, or real websocket/network traces, so evidence is limited to assertion-backed Flutter logs.
frictions.resolution: Saved one expanded log per targeted command under the mission evidence directory and recorded the harness limitation directly in each assertion evidence block.

frictions.description: Notification validation for this milestone is explicitly foreground-only, so background or terminated-app delivery was out of scope for the assigned assertions even though the contract titles mention notification surfacing broadly.
frictions.resolution: Evaluated only active-app notification routing, stale suppression, and dedup behavior per the hardening-mobile-integration guidance.
